title: Chili Oil

ingredients.title: Chili Oil Base:
items: 1 cup neutral oil (vegetable, peanut, or grapeseed oil)
1/4 cup crushed chili flakes (adjust based on heat preference, Sichuan or Korean chili works well)

instruction.title: Prepare the aromatics
steps: In a small saucepan, combine the oil with garlic, ginger, shallot, star anise, cinnamon, bay leaves, and Sichuan peppercorns. Heat over low-medium heat for about 10–15 minutes, stirring occasionally. The goal is to infuse the oil without burning the aromatics.

instruction.title: Strain the aromatics (optional)
steps: For a clear oil, strain the aromatics and discard them. For a rustic style, leave them in.
Place chili flakes, sugar, salt, and sesame seeds in a heatproof bowl.

instruction.title: Heat the oil to infuse the chili
steps: Heat the infused oil until it’s hot but not smoking (~275–300°F / 135–150°C). Carefully pour the hot oil over the chili mixture. It should sizzle immediately, releasing a fragrant aroma.

instruction.title: Cool and store
steps: Let the chili oil cool to room temperature, then transfer to a clean jar. It can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 1 month. The flavors will deepen over time.

chefTips: Balance heat and aroma: Use half toasted chili flakes and half raw for layered flavor.
Use high-quality oil: A neutral oil with a clean taste lets the spices shine.
Infuse slowly: Low and slow heating prevents bitterness from burned spices.
Optional finishing touches: Add finely chopped fried shallots, garlic chips, or crushed toasted peanuts for texture.
